judge_output.rationale: Both required findings are fully matched in the agent's final tree. The Death fact (f1) is recorded with the correct date of 23 December 1883, and the Burial fact (f2) is recorded with the correct date of 31 December 1883 and location of Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway. The agent successfully recovered both stripped facts from the research process and populated them into the target person (K87G-T89) with appropriate source citations.

proof_quality.rationale: The proof summary demonstrates exhaustive search across five record categories (Burials, Church Books, Census 1865, Census 1875, Probate) plus FAN search (Census 1891) and documents access limitations to the original kirkebok image. No conflicts required resolution. However, corroboration is limited: the conclusion rests on two derivative indexes (Burials and Church Books) that are both derived from the same underlying Stavanger Domkirke kirkebok—the agent correctly identifies this as 'a single evidence unit' rather than independent sources. The 1865 and 1891 census entries provide indirect corroboration of identity and location, but not of the death or burial date itself. The declared tier of 'probable' is appropriate given reliance on derivative sources without access to the original kirkebok, and the narrative appropriately explains why 'proved' status was not reached. The proof quality is partial rather than strong because the primary evidence (the burial date itself) comes from a single derivative source unit, though the overall narrative is thorough and methodologically sound.

entry.question: When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 1817, Stavanger, Norway) die, and where was he buried?
entry.rationale: This directly addresses the research objective. Norwegian ministerial books (kirkebøker) recorded deaths and burials together in a single register entry, so one search type should yield both facts. Stavanger diocese records on FamilySearch and Digitalarkivet are the primary sources.

args.query: Norway death burial church records genealogy

entry.rationale: Norway, Burials, 1666-1927 (collection 1468081) is the primary indexed burial database for Norway. Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland, born 1817 Stavanger, should appear here if he died in a Lutheran parish between 1840 and 1910. The index typically records name, death/burial date, age, and place of residence, directly answering the question.

Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958 (collection 4237104) is the comprehensive indexed-plus-images collection of Norwegian kirkebøker, covering births, marriages, deaths, and burials. Searching here captures any entry missed by the narrower Burials collection, and provides access to original images showing the full burial register entry including burial parish.

The 1865 Norwegian census (collection 3756102) will show whether Kristian Fredrik was alive in 1865 and record his residence at that date, narrowing the search window for his death and identifying the parish whose church books should be examined. Born 1817, he would be ~48 in 1865.
The 1875 Norwegian census (collection 1529106) will confirm whether he was still alive in 1875 (~age 58) and where he was living, further narrowing the death date window and death location.

Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640-1903 (collection 1661306) may contain an estate record naming the date and place of his death. Norwegian probate (skifte) records were kept when a person of means died, and they record the decedent's name, death date, and residence — corroborating evidence for the death date and parish found in church records.

Digitalarkivet (digitalarkivet.no) hosts images of Stavanger parish kirkebøker (Stavanger Domkirke and surrounding parishes). If the indexed FamilySearch searches fail to find him, browsing the Stavanger death/burial registers directly on Digitalarkivet is the most reliable fallback, as Stavanger was his last-known location and his burial likely occurred there.

FAN item — if Kristian Fredrik died before 1891, searching the 1891 census (collection 4067726) for his widow, children, or other household members may provide clues about when and where he died, and where the family was thereafter. This places the FAN cluster in their community context following his presumed death.

entry.notes: Index entry derived from Norwegian Lutheran church burial registers (kirkebøker) for Stavanger. Name indexed as 'Christian Frederick H. Birkeland' — likely a transcription variant of 'Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland'. Original church book images available via Digitalarkivet (digitalarkivet.no).

entry.informant: unknown reporter to parish at time of death registration

fields.informant_bias_notes: Death date recorded in burial register completed 31 Dec 1883, eight days after the 23 Dec death. The informant who provided the death date to the pastor is not identified in this index entry. Could have been a family member present at the death, an attending physician, or a neighbor. Proximity and first-hand knowledge cannot be confirmed from this derivative source.

Birth year 1817 reported at burial registration in December 1883 — 66 years after the birth. No informant present at the 1883 registration could plausibly have witnessed the 1817 birth. Information classified secondary regardless of who the specific informant was. Birth year is indirect evidence for q_001: it corroborates the identity of this burial record as belonging to the research subject (Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland, born 1817), which is an inferential step.

entry.rationale: The burial index gives the name 'Christian Frederick H. Birkeland' — a standard 19th-century transcription of 'Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland' ('Christian'/'Kristian' and 'Frederick'/'Fredrik' are common English/Norwegian cognate pairs; 'Tollefsen' as patronymic would not always appear in burial index). Surname 'Birkeland' is an exact match. Burial year 1883 aligns with K87G-T89's last-known active period (appears in records through 1880). Birth year on the same record is 1817 (a_004), which matches K87G-T89 exactly. The record received a 5-star FamilySearch tree match to K87G-T89 during search triage. All identifying factors agree; this is the same individual.

Death date 23 December 1883 extracted from the burial register entry for 'Christian Frederick H. Birkeland', identified as K87G-T89 via name variant, birth year 1817, burial location Stavanger, and 5-star FamilySearch tree match. All identifying factors agree; this death date belongs to K87G-T89.

Burial date 31 December 1883 and burial place Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway extracted from the same burial register entry as a_001 and a_002. Stavanger matches K87G-T89's known long-term residence. The 5-star tree match and all identifying factors confirm this burial entry belongs to K87G-T89. This assertion directly answers q_001 (where was Kristian Fredrik buried).

Birth year 1817 stated in the burial register entry for the deceased. K87G-T89 is documented as born 11 July 1817 in Norway. The birth year is an exact match and corroborates identity alongside the name variant and Stavanger burial place. Classified as indirect evidence supporting identity for q_001.

This indicates collection 1529106 has no indexed records for Norwegian 1875 census — likely a collection coverage/indexing gap rather than evidence of subject's absence. Nil result is NOT meaningful evidence of death before 1875; it reflects collection incompleteness. Stavanger 1875 census images are available on Digitalarkivet.no but this FamilySearch collection appears not to index them. The 1865 census (log_003) establishes the subject was alive in 1865; the 1883 burial records (log_001, log_002) establish he died 23 Dec 1883. The 1875 window cannot be bracketed from this collection.

entry.informant_bias_notes: FAN evidence: K87G-T89's daughter living in Stavanger in 1891, 8 years after subject's death and burial in Stavanger. Indirect evidence for q_001 as it corroborates Stavanger as the family's community and burial location.

entry.rationale: Name 'Marthe Marie Grude, f. Birkeland' in 1891 Stavanger census. 'f. Birkeland' = født Birkeland (born Birkeland = maiden name Birkeland). Born 1844, Stavanger. Married status. This matches LCX7-DTC (Martha Marie Christiansen Birkeland, born 14 Mar 1844). The 1891 census birthplace 'Stavanger Bÿ' vs. tree's 'Klepp' is a secondary discrepancy. Core identifiers (given names, birth year, maiden surname) match LCX7-DTC.

args.prompt:
You are the GPS senior genealogist reviewer. Please perform a pre-exhaustiveness evaluation of question q_001 in this research project.

Project directory: /private/var/folders/ys/gs7l5hcx1rs6dvjt0ys_n05h0000gn/T/e2e-birkeland-death-1883-s2korvez

Research question q_001: "When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 1817, Stavanger, Norway) die, and where was he buried?"

Context: A previous pre-exhaustiveness evaluation (ev_001, timestamp 2026-07-01T16:00:00Z) returned verdict "address_first" with three must_address items:
1. Execute the planned items (pli_002–pli_007) — Standard 19/GPS Component 1
2. Consult original church book image via Digitalarkivet (pli_006) — Standard 32
3. Document nil results — Standard 13

Since that evaluation, the following work has been done:
- pli_002 (Norway Church Books 1797-1958, collection 4237104): POSITIVE — found "Christian Frederik Birkeland", burial 31 Dec 1883 Stavanger, death 23 Dec 1883, born 1817 Stavanger. Record ARK ark:/61903/1:1:68QQ-PP3H. Original kirkebok image ARK identified: https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM (documented in src_002 notes). Log: log_002.
- pli_003 (Norway Census 1865, collection 3756102): POSITIVE — found "Christian F.T. Birkeland" (initials = Fredrik Tollefsen), male, born 1816 Stavanger, married, household includes wife "Serine" (born 1817 Time/Rogaland) and children Tollef C. (1840) and Hanna S. (1854). Confirms alive in Stavanger 1865. Log: log_003.
- pli_004 (Norway Census 1875, collection 1529106): NEGATIVE — collection has no indexed records for Norway at all under this surname (coverage gap, not evidence of absence). Log: log_004.
- pli_005 (Norway Probate Index 1640-1903, collection 1661306): NEGATIVE — no probate found. Meaningful nil: by December 1883 all surviving children were adults (Martha ~39, Hanna ~26); no minor heirs, so probate not required under Norwegian law. Log: log_005.
- pli_006 (Digitalarkivet, original kirkebok): PARTIAL — original image ARK https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM identified via record_read, but not directly readable (image_read tool requires DGS NUMBER_NUMBER format, not 3:1: ARK format). Image URL documented in src_002. Log: log_006.
- pli_007 (Norway Census 1891, collection 4067726, FAN): POSITIVE — found "Marthe Marie Grude, f. Birkeland", born 1844 Stavanger, married, residing Stavanger 1891. Identified as K87G-T89's daughter LCX7-DTC. Confirms family continued in Stavanger post-1883. Log: log_007.

Current evidence summary:
- src_001 (collection 1468081): "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland", death 23 Dec 1883, burial 31 Dec 1883, Stavanger — derivative index
- src_002 (collection 4237104): "Christian Frederik Birkeland", death 23 Dec 1883, burial 31 Dec 1883, Stavanger — derivative index (same underlying kirkebok, different FamilySearch collection)
- src_003 (collection 3756102): "Christian F.T. Birkeland" in Stavanger 1865 census — confirms alive 1865
- src_004 (collection 4067726): daughter "Marthe Marie Grude, f. Birkeland" in Stavanger 1891 — FAN
- Original kirkebok image ARK documented but not transcribed

Please evaluate whether the research is now ready to proceed to exhaustiveness declaration, or if there are remaining must_address items. Focus on:
1. Whether the Standard 19 requirement is met (reasonably exhaustive search)
2. Whether Standard 32 (original over derivative) is adequately addressed given the image ARK was identified but not transcribed
3. Whether identity resolution is sound with current evidence
4. Whether the death date 23 December 1883 and burial 31 December 1883 at Stavanger are adequately supported

Write the evaluation to: evaluations/pre-exhaustiveness-q_001-2026-07-01T17-00-00.json
And append an entry to research.json evaluations[] section using the research_append tool.

The evaluation JSON should follow the same structure as the existing evaluation at evaluations/pre-exhaustiveness-q_001-2026-07-01T16-00-00.json.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched burial indexes in two FamilySearch collections (1468081 and 4237104), confirming death 23 December 1883 and burial 31 December 1883 in Stavanger. 1865 census independently corroborates identity via household composition (wife Serine, son Tollef, daughter Hanna). Probate nil documented as meaningful (adult surviving children, no minor heirs). 1891 FAN census confirms family remained in Stavanger post-1883. Original kirkebok image identified (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM, documented in src_002) but not accessed due to automated toolset limitations (image_read incompatible with 3:1: ARK format; WebFetch blocked); good-faith access attempts documented in log_006 and log_008. Overturn risk from unread original is very low: both derivative indexes derive from and agree exactly on the same kirkebok entry; the original would add informant/cause-of-death fields but would not change the death date or burial location conclusion.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes. Two derivative indexes from two FamilySearch collections independently confirm: death 23 December 1883, burial 31 December 1883, Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, for a person named Christian Frederick/Frederik Birkeland born 1817 — matching K87G-T89 on all identifying attributes.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Five record categories searched: burial indexes (Norway Burials 1666-1927 and Norway Church Books 1797-1958), census (1865 positive, 1875 nil/coverage gap, 1891 FAN positive), and probate (meaningful nil). Name variants tried (Kristian/Christian, Fredrik/Frederik). Stavanger and Rogaland jurisdictions covered.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Documented limitation: original Stavanger Domkirke kirkebok image is accessible to human researchers on Digitalarkivet.no (image ARK ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM documented in src_002) but could not be opened by automated toolset. Multiple access attempts made and logged (log_006, log_008). Overturn risk is very low: both derivatives derive from and agree exactly on the same original.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Two independent FamilySearch collection searches (different indexes of the same underlying kirkebok) plus the 1865 census as a genuinely independent source. Note: src_001 and src_002 are derivative indexes of the SAME kirkebok — they provide one evidence unit for the burial facts, not two independent sources. The 1865 census (src_003) provides true independence: it establishes identity and Stavanger residence from a different record type, different informant, and different institutional origin.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: src_001 and src_002 are derivative indexes (no original with primary information was accessed). The 1865 census (src_003) is a derivative index of an original census — the enumerator's direct record of household presence constitutes primary information for the residence fact. No original record with primary death information was consulted; this is the documented limitation of this automated run.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Minor discrepancy: 1865 census records birth year 1816 vs. established 1817 (±1 typical census tolerance). Resolved by preponderance: burial records (two indexes) and tree documentation agree on 1817. No conflicts affecting the death date or burial location.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. Death date, burial date, and burial location are consistently reported across two indexes of the same kirkebok. The 1865 census independently places the subject in Stavanger (consistent with later Stavanger burial). The 1891 FAN census shows the family remained in Stavanger. No contrary evidence identified. The unread original kirkebok image might add informant and cause-of-death but is extremely unlikely to change the date or location conclusion.

Access limitation documented in log_006 and log_008. Research declared exhaustive within automated toolset limits. Overturn risk from unread original is very low.
entry.narrative_markdown:
## The Death and Burial of Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (1817–1883)

**Research question:** When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 1817, Stavanger) die, and where was he buried?

**Conclusion:** It is probable that Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland died on **23 December 1883** and was buried on **31 December 1883** in **Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway**, in the Stavanger Domkirke parish.

### Evidence Summary

**Burial index entries (src_001 and src_002 — one evidence unit)**

Two FamilySearch databases—*Norway, Burials, 1666–1927* (collection 1468081) and *Norway, Church Books, 1797–1958* (collection 4237104)—both return an entry for a man named "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland" / "Christian Frederik Birkeland" who died 23 December 1883 and was buried 31 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, with birth year 1817.¹² Both indexes are **derivative** sources derived from the same underlying Stavanger Domkirke kirkebok; they constitute a single evidence unit (confirming transcription fidelity between two separate indexing projects), not two independent sources of the burial fact. The burial event itself (date and place recorded by the officiating pastor) carries **primary** information quality (official_duty informant); the death date is **indeterminate** since neither index entry names who reported it to the pastor.

**Identity: the burial record person is K87G-T89**

The name spellings "Christian Frederick" and "Christian Frederik" are standard 19th-century Danish-Norwegian and English cognates for the Norwegian "Kristian Fredrik." The patronymic "Tollefsen" is not given in either index, consistent with Norwegian practice of omitting patronymics in burial headings once a heritable surname (Birkeland) was established. The burial birth year of 1817 matches K87G-T89's documented birth year exactly. The burial location—Stavanger, Rogaland—matches K87G-T89's long-term established residence. The FamilySearch engine assigned a 5-star tree match to K87G-T89 during triage. All identifying attributes agree.

**1865 Norwegian census (src_003 — independent corroboration of identity)**

A search of *Norway, Census, 1865* (FamilySearch collection 3756102) returned an entry for "Christian F.T. Birkeland," male, born 1816, married, residing Stavanger, Rogaland, 1865 (ark:/61903/1:1:DXC9-BG6Z).³ The initials "F.T." match "Fredrik Tollefsen." The 1865 household independently corroborates identity through three named relatives matching K87G-T89's known family: wife "Serine" (born ~1817, Løye/Time, Rogaland—matching Serine Kristoffersdatter Aasland), son "Tollef C." (born ~1840), and daughter "Hanna S." (born 1854). This household composition—drawn from a record type, informant, and institutional origin entirely separate from the burial registers—strengthens identity from "confident on one source" to "confident on independent record types." The census places K87G-T89 alive in Stavanger in 1865, establishing he died after that date. This source is **derivative** (index of enumerator schedules); the residence fact carries **primary** information quality (enumerator's direct observation); evidence for q_001 is **indirect** (proves identity and brackets the death window rather than stating the death date directly).

*Minor discrepancy:* The 1865 census records birth year 1816 versus 1817 in the burial records. A ±1-year difference is within the typical recording tolerance for 19th-century Norwegian census entries. The burial records (supported by two indexing projects independently agreeing on 1817) carry greater weight on this specific point. The discrepancy does not affect the death or burial date conclusion.

**1891 FAN census (src_004 — indirect corroboration of burial location)**

K87G-T89's daughter Marthe Marie Birkeland (married name Grude) appears in the 1891 Norwegian census (FamilySearch collection 4067726) still residing in Stavanger (ark:/61903/1:1:DVMP-VD3Z).⁴ Her continued Stavanger residence eight years after her father's death corroborates Stavanger as the Birkeland family's established community and the plausibility of his burial there. This source is **derivative**; the residence fact is **primary** (enumerator's direct observation); evidence for q_001 is **indirect**.

**Probate nil — meaningful absence**

A search of *Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640–1903* (FamilySearch collection 1661306) returned no estate record for any variant of Kristian Fredrik Birkeland.⁵ This nil is assessed as meaningful: K87G-T89's adult surviving children (Martha, born ~1844; Hanna, born ~1857) were approximately 39 and 26 at the time of his 1883 death—no minor heirs requiring guardianship or a mandatory Norwegian *skifte* proceeding. The absence of probate is consistent with the evidence.

**Original kirkebok image — documented access limitation**

The original Stavanger Domkirke kirkebok image underlying both index entries was identified via the collection 4237104 record: FamilySearch ARK https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM (documented in src_002). Multiple good-faith access attempts were made: the `image_read` tool does not support the modern 3:1: ARK format; `volume_search` returned no Stavanger burial register volumes for 1880–1895; `external_links_search` located the Digitalarkivet browse URL but automated access was blocked; a direct fetch to Digitalarkivet was also blocked.⁶ The image is accessible to human researchers at the URL above and on Digitalarkivet.no; it would add informant, cause-of-death, and age-at-death fields but is extremely unlikely to change the date or burial location. This access limitation is the primary reason this conclusion reaches "probable" rather than "proved."

### Tier Declaration

**Probable.** The death date (23 December 1883) and burial place (Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway; burial date 31 December 1883) rest on a single evidence unit—two derivative burial indexes of the same Stavanger Domkirke kirkebok—with identity independently corroborated by the 1865 Norwegian census household and indirectly by the 1891 FAN census. No original record with primary death information was consulted, preventing the GPS "proved" standard (which requires two or more independent original sources with primary information, all conflicts resolved, and exhaustive research). Research was declared reasonably exhaustive within automated toolset limits; overturn risk from the unread kirkebok image is very low. A researcher with access to Digitalarkivet.no or the FamilySearch viewer can advance this conclusion to "proved" by consulting and transcribing the original burial register entry.

---

¹ "Norway, Burials, 1666-1927," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:NWVC-G5W : accessed 1 July 2026), entry for Christian Frederick H. Birkeland, 1883.
² "Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:68QQ-PP3H : accessed 1 July 2026), entry for Christian Frederik Birkeland, 31 December 1883; image ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM.
³ "Norway, Census, 1865," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:DXC9-BG6Z : accessed 1 July 2026), entry for Christian F. T. Birkeland, Stavanger, Rogaland, 1865.
⁴ "Norway, Census, 1891," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:DVMP-VD3Z : accessed 1 July 2026), entry for Marthe Marie Grude, f. Birkeland, Stavanger, 1891.
⁵ Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640–1903 (FamilySearch collection 1661306): negative search; no entry for Kristian Fredrik / Christian Frederik Birkeland.
⁶ Access attempts documented in research log entries log_006 and log_008.
